item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: We got an early look at three of the biggest games coming to Switch 2. This is our verdict
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: Telstra records billion dollar profit, reveals it shed 2356 jobs in 2025
item.description: T﻿he job cuts have saved $118 million alone.

item.title: eBay to buy Depop in $1.7b move to reach Gen Z and Millennials
item.description: e﻿Bay hopes buying Depop will help it reach a younger and more fashion-forward audience.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Australians aren't eating enough burritos. It just cost one company hundreds of millions of dollars
item.description: I﻿ts share price has dropped by more than half in the last 12 months.

item.title: Employees at Aussie start-up Eucalyptus set to receive windfall after $1.6 billion acquisition
item.description: Employee shareholders at an Aussie start-up are set for a big pay day after the company was acquired for $1.6 billion.

item.title: WiseTech Global set to cut up to 2000 jobs for 'AI efficiency'
item.description: T﻿he software company said ongoing developments in AI would deliver a leaner organisation.

item.title: Domino's Pizza sheds 10 per cent of customers after reducing discounts
item.description: Domino's started offering fewer discounts, and some of its customers weren't too happy.

item.title: Man fired after pouring cooking oil down the drain outside popular bakery
item.description: The incident quickly gained attention, with members of the community saying the man's actions were "pure laziness", "disgusting", and ﻿"such a shame".

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.title: Company behind Australian cultural icon Hills Hoist could soon be sold
item.description: The company behind one of Australia's most iconic brands is set to be put up for sale as its American owner focuses on operations closer to home.

item.title: Almost 40,000 Budget Direct customers allegedly overcharged $3.3 million
item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: VB pins hopes on mid-strength as Aussies drop classic beers
item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap is creeping down, but some major Australian employers are lagging
item.description: Many major Australian brands are failing to turn the dial on the gender pay gap, with some even slipping backwards in the past year, a detailed new report has revealed.

item.title: Aussie pubs, jobs safe for now as UK company lays off hundreds of staff
item.description: The company has four breweries and about 100 pubs around the globe, including five here in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Business owner sends premier $24,000 bill after new public holiday announced
item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.title: Rail union calls for free public transport as fuel crisis continues
item.description: Australians are paying about $30 more for a tank of unleaded petrol, and $70 for a tank of diesel.

item.title: One Nation to join South Australian lower house after surging support in election
item.description: One Nation has decimated the Liberals and claimed its first lower house seat won at an election outside of Queensland.

item.description: It comes after Trump threatened to send in immigration officers amid a funding shutdown.

item.title: How many days does Australia have left of fuel?
item.description: The government has insisted there is no issue of fuel supply, but how much fuel is actually left in Australia?

item.description: Motorists are being encouraged to use a cheaper alternative at the bowser amid concern about fuel prices﻿ skyrocketing.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies aged 43
item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.title: Cheaper cars, forbidden names, and more trade for farmers: Australia finalises long-awaited $10 billion-a-year
item.description: E﻿ight years after negotiations started, Canberra has signed a free-trade deal with the European Union.

item.title: Man charged for allegedly smashing NSW Parliament House window with barbell
item.description: A man has been arrested after a NSW Parliament House window was smashed with a metal barbell early ﻿this morning.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: Experts liken sky-high fuel prices to three interest rate rises
item.description: Experts are warning the economic shock from sky-high fuel prices is the equivalent of three interest rate rises.

item.title: Fines for dodgy petrol pricing doubled as fuel crisis continues
item.description: T﻿he government is trying to respond to growing anxiety about the fuel crisis sparked by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: 'Businesses are going under now': Transport body knocks Albo's fuel crisis plan
item.description: T﻿he peak body for road transport says fresh national measures to combat the burgeoning fuel crisis fail to address the immediate needs for struggling operators.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.description: The figures don't paint an overly pretty picture for Australia – although the government says there's a good reason for it.

item.title: Victoria announces sweeping changes to rental laws
item.description: Sweeping changes to Victorian rental laws will kick in next week, making it easier - and a little cheaper - for prospective tenants.

item.title: Government to use taxpayer funds to underwrite cost of petrol in bid to ease fuel crisis
item.description: T﻿he move is a bid to shore up fuel supply and bring down the cost of petrol.

item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.title: Poll shows Australians blame Donald Trump for fuel crisis
item.description: P﻿rices at the pump are hurting voters and a huge majority of them are pointing the finger at US President Donald Trump.

item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: What is the 'sovereign citizen' movement?
item.description: It's the belief system of the alleged Porepunkah cop killer, but it has a long and disturbing history.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said the landmark ruling was a "great outcome" for workers across key sectors.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.title: RBA to end card surcharges as cash usage dwindles
item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.title: Mother-of-three at risk of deportation after decade-old visa mistake
item.description: "I just want safety, stay here, take care of my family."

item.title: 'Doesn't pass the pub test': Hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges
item.description: Inside the hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges.

item.title: 'It has been a privilege': Cusack's Furniture to close late 2026
item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: Phones, smartwatches and earbuds to be banned in every Victorian school after government extends device crackd
item.description: Victoria's nation-first phone ban was first introduced to government schools in 2020 and will now capture all schools, including private and Catholic, from January 28, 2027.

item.title: Three-month fuel excise cost passes Senate in bid to fight high prices
item.description: Fuel price relief has been made official but drivers are warned it will take time for the excise cut to be felt in their wallets.

item.title: As fuel excise set to be halved, when will fuel prices in Australia go down?
item.description: With key taxes being slashed, when will Australians finally see relief at the pump?

item.title: Western Australia enacts emergency fuel powers as PM urges calm
item.description: The state's six fuel suppliers will have 48 hours to hand over "sensitive information", or risk being fined up to $100,000 per day.

item.title: PM defends lacklustre national address
item.description: The speech is widely rumoured to involve financial assistance for businesses hit by soaring fuel costs.

item.title: What our readers thought of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's address to the nation
item.description: "A waste of time" – those were the words nine.com.au readers used over and over again.

item.title: Neo-Nazi forced offline after alleged threats to MPs
item.description: Joel Davis, 31, has been in custody on remand since his arrest in November for allegedly calling for the "rhetorical rape" of independent Wentworth MP Allegra Spender.

item.title: We were all set for prime time, then we were let down with a thud by our leaders
item.description: Opinion: In terms of doom and gloom, we're being bombarded at the moment with bad news.

item.title: New COVID strain circulating in Australia as it spreads across the globe
item.description: Experts say there are no signs the variant BA.3.2, otherwise known as "Cicada", poses a bigger health threat than other variations.

item.title: Shocking report blows the lid on where government funding is being spent
item.description: What's shocking is that 68 per cent of operators are unlicensed and one of those organisations received nearly $34 million in funding last financial year.

item.title: Spending cuts, tax reforms flagged in next month's federal budget
item.description: More and more Australians are draining﻿ their savings just to stay afloat as hopes for cost-of-living relief in next month's federal budget slowly fade.

item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.title: Experts send dire fuel crisis warning as PM travels to Singapore to lock in supplies
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is travelling to Singapore in a bid to shore up Australia's fuel supply, trading on the country's reputation as a food bowl.

item.description: The rapper has drawn widespread condemnation for making antisemitic remarks and voicing admiration for Adolf Hitler.

item.title: VIktor Orban offers to be 'mouse' to Vladimir Putin's 'lion' in reportedly leaked phone call
item.description: V﻿iktor Orban offered to be the "mouse" to the Russian president's "lion", the reported transcripts read.

item.title: Nationals leader Matt Canavan unveils plan to create an 'Australia on steroids'
item.description: Matt Canavan has called for new cities, more babies and a domestic space industry.

item.description: Officers first detected a motorcycle speeding along the Princes Freeway﻿ in Lara around 10pm yesterday.

item.description: Australia produces very little oil itself, getting most of it from Asian neighbours, but David Crisafulli wants that to change.

item.description: A﻿ustralian tech giant Canva has snapped up two local artificial intelligence (AI) companies. But bigger news is on the way.

item.description: Payslip complacency has reached remarkable highs.

item.description: The resignations have triggered a cabinet reshuffle months ahead of Victoria's state election.

item.description: A state's political donation and campaign funding laws have been ruled constitutionally invalid, in a case that could have national implications.

item.title: Virgin to cut flights, raise costs after $40 million fuel hit
item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.title: Australia signs key deal with Brunei amid fuel crisis, recession fears
item.description: Australia has signed a deal with Asian neighbour Brunei that will get more fertiliser to farmers at a time where they continue to be smashed by the fuel crisis.

item.description: A surveillance photo of a bank robber was broadcast on the 11pm television news in the United States on April 19, 1995.

item.description: T﻿he US president has issued a stern warning over attacks on ships in the key waterway.

item.description: T﻿he pontiff says his 'tyrants' speech was not directed at Donald Trump.

item.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves six dead before police shot and killed the gunman
item.description: A gunman killed six people in the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, before he was shot and killed by police, authorities said.

item.title: Caribbean nation investigates 56 bodies dumped at a cemetery
item.description: M﻿ost of the human remains were from children, authorities say.

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
item.description: North Korea launched multiple ballistic missiles toward the sea on Sunday, its neighbors said, the North’s latest weapons testing activity this year.

item.title: California bear-suit luxury car scam ends in insurance fraud sentences for trio
item.description: A group sent videos of the bear trashing the Rolls-Royce and two Mercedes. A biologist was called in to check it out.

item.title: Trump says US negotiators will be in Pakistan on Monday for talks with Iran
item.description: He accused Iran of violating the ceasefire agreement by firing bullets in the Strait of Hormuz and threatened to destroy civilian infrastructure in Iran.

item.title: UK police investigating if arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies
item.description: ASIO famously came out publicly with claims Iran orchestrated at least two alleged ﻿antisemitic firebombings on Australian soil last year by hiring criminals to do its bidding.

item.title: Big hint Apple's first foldable smartphone is coming this year
item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.title: Israeli Defence Forces confirms photo of soldier smashing a statue of Jesus Christ in Lebanon is real
item.description: T﻿he IDF is now investigating, claiming the incident is "is wholly inconsistent with the values expected of its troops."

item.title: Jordan Peterson suffering from 'horrible' neurological injury induced by medication, his daughter says
item.description: T﻿he daughter of the prominent commentator said it was caused by medication.

item.title: 'We've certainly seen unpredictability': Wong agrees with Trump
item.description: The foreign minister stopped short of saying the president's nature would blow any plans for long term peace.

item.title: Louisiana gunman killed eight children, including seven of his own, police say, in deadliest mass shooting sin
item.description: T﻿he tragedy was the deadliest gun rampage in the United States for more than two years.

item.title: Tsunami alert, mega-quake advisory after magnitude 7.7 earthquake strikes off Japan
item.description: Officials said the advisory was not a quake prediction but urged residents to raise their preparedness, such as emergency food and their grab bag just in case, while continuing their daily lives.

item.title: Singer D4vd charged with murder of girl, 14, found dismembered in his car
item.description: The long-dead body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, 14, was found inside a Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills on September 8, one day after she would have turned 15.

item.title: Shooting at historic tourist pyramid in Mexico leaves one dead, multiple injured
item.description: A﻿ famous historical site in Mexico City, popular with tourists, has been rocked by tragedy.

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel sues The Atlantic for $348 million over claims of drunkenness
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel is suing magazine The Atlantic for $US250 million (A$348 million) over reports of his alleged drunkenness.

item.title: Third woman leaves Trump's cabinet in two months
item.description: Scandal-plagued US Secretary of Labour Lori Chavez-DeRemer has resigned, the third woman to leave Trump's cabinet in the past two months.

item.title: Gas tax would make Australia $17 billion and cut domestic prices, Senate told
item.description: Norway and Qatar have done it. Now there's a growing push for Australia to make the move that "solves all those problems".

item.title: Why there's real risk war will now spin out of control
item.description: As a precarious ceasefire nears the US president has delivered an ominous warning to the nation's generals.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down after 15 years
item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.title: What to know as ceasefire in the Iran war hangs in the balance
item.description: Neither the US nor Iran have publicly confirmed timing of any new talks or plans to extend the ceasefire, but sudden changes have been the norm in the lead-up to past rounds of talks.

item.title: As US-Iran ceasefire deadline nears, uncertainty hangs over possible talks
item.description: Last-minute ceasefire talks between the United States and Iran looked uncertain, despite earlier signals from regional officials suggesting they'd hold a new round of ceasefire talks in Islamabad.

item.title: Royals family remember Queen Elizabeth II on what would have been her 100th birthday
item.description: Britain's royal family marked what would have been the late Queen Elizabeth II's 100th birthday by unveiling comprehensive plans for a new charity and memorial in central London.

item.title: Today in History - April 22: The complex operation to rescue dozens of hostages in barely 20 minutes
item.description: A four-month long hostage crisis in Peru came to a violent end in a remarkably complex operation on April 22, 1997.

item.title: UK to introduce lifelong smoking ban for anyone born after 2008
item.description: Children aged 17 and under will be banned from ever legally purchasing cigarettes in their lifetime under new legislation in Britain.

item.title: Ukrainian officials propose naming region after Donald Trump to win over the president
item.description: Officials in Ukraine have discussed naming a disputed section of their country after Donald Trump, in a bid to win the US president's favour, the New York Times reports.

item.title: 'Should never have happened': Minister admits failings after two foster children allowed to live with serial k
item.description: "It was closed based on unverified information about Arthurell and his possible incapacity," the minister admitted.

item.title: 'We've been sold out': Influencer's impassioned plea to Senate
item.description: K﻿onrad Benjamin, a former teacher who founded Punter's Politics, let loose before a Senate committee yesterday.

item.title: Ballistic missile paraded through Iranian streets
item.description: The missile can hit targets up to 2000km away, and can carry a warhead weighing nearly two tonnes.

item.title: 'ATM for shonks, grifters, fraudsters and crooks': Government flags crackdown, cost-cutting in NDIS
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler delivered the sweeping changes in a National Press Club Address and said the NDIS will no longer ﻿"be an ATM for shonks, grifters, fraudsters and crooks".

item.title: Over 200 million litres of extra diesel coming to Australia
item.description: P﻿rime Minister Anthony Albanese said Australian buyers had secured four extra cargo loads.

item.title: Iran fires on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz as US maintains blockade and diplomacy stalls
item.description: The standoff between the US and Iran has effectively choked off nearly all exports through the strait, through which 20 per cent of the world's traded oil passes in peacetime, with no end in sight.

item.title: How a crummy cake kicked off a food-delivery crisis in China
item.description: T﻿he inquiry has been marked by scuffles between investigators and delivery service employees, a feigned medical emergency and hastily scribbled notes to "stay silent".

item.title: The fun photos of Harry and Meghan that could land Aussie small businesses in hot water
item.description: They seem harmless upon first glance, but look a little closer and you'll see the truth.

item.title: Today in History - April 23: Notorious assassin spared execution by actions of completely unconnected killer
item.description: The murderer of Robert F Kennedy, Sirhan Sirhan, was sentenced to death on April 23, 1969.

item.title: Donald Trump's bid to secure power for his party just backfired big time
item.description: In July last year, Donald Trump began a campaign that he hoped would lock up his party's control in Congress.

item.title: Trump's Secretary of the Navy abruptly leaving - the latest sudden departure from the Pentagon
item.description: Donald Trump's Secretary of the Navy is leaving his post "effectively immediately", the Pentagon has announced.

item.title: CEO of Trump's Truth Social media company ousted after losing billions
item.description: Donald Trump's social media company has ousted its CEO after its share price fell by nearly 75 per cent in just two years.

item.title: 'There should be consequences': Katter United Party calls for jail time for people who burn the Australian fla
item.description: "That flag represents our people, our freedoms, and every Australian who has stood under it to serve."

item.title: Trump envoy asks president to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup
item.description: One of Donald Trump's top envoys has asked the president to replace Iran's team in the FIFA World Cup with Italy.

item.title: FBoy Island star, aspiring politicians fined for placing bets on themselves
item.description: A popular betting website has banned three US politicians for placing bets on the outcomes of their own elections.

item.title: Why Trump is losing his grip and what can turn it around
item.description: Since peace talks between the US and Iran were cancelled, Trump has offered little clarity on what's next.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith 'never planned' to flee overseas
item.description: Documents detail plans made with his partner to open a business overseas but she says he always intended to return home if criminal charges were laid.

item.title: Meta to roll out feature giving parents access to their child's AI search topics on Facebook, Instagram
item.description: The social media giant is rolling out changes to how parents can access their child's Meta AI search history while using supervision mode.

item.title: 'Very difficult': Actor's teary court claim about Rebel
item.description: Charlotte MacInnes choked back tears as she described Hollywood actor Rebel Wilson as "very difficult to be around" amid a dispute over an alleged sexual harassment complaint.

item.title: Australia has more fuel than before the war but it's coming from very different places
item.description: N﻿ew figures from the government show just how drastically the war in Iran has changed Australia's fuel supplies.

item.title: Trump orders US military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats choking Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump ordered the US military to intensify its mine clearing efforts in the Strait of Hormuz, with permission to get rid of any vessels standing in their way.

item.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount's $113.5 billion takeover of the Hollywood giant
item.description: Many critics have sounded the alarm on joining two of Hollywood's biggest remaining five studios.

item.title: Today in History - April 24: World's biggest band nearly reunited for tiny sum
item.description: A public reunion of the world's best-loved songwriting duo nearly took place the night of April 24, 1976.

item.title: Victorian father accused of threatening to blow up Perth Airport shouts in court
item.description: He could face 10 years in jail for allegedly saying "I am going to blow this place up" but swears his words were misrepresented.

item.title: Pair charged over $13 million drugs intercept
item.description: The man and woman are accused of hiding the drugs in an SUV intercepted by police as it was driving from NSW to Victoria.

item.title: Tech giant Microsoft unveiles $25 billion plan to reshape the Australian workplace
item.description: A﻿nd the man in charge says Australia is in a prime position for the major growth technology.

item.title: Perth student Alexander Philogene, 21, dies hours after developing meningococcal B symptoms on flight
item.description: Australians have been warned against a severe strain of meningococcal B circulating in Europe after a Perth man died from the deadly disease while on university exchange.

item.title: A 'super El Niño' looks likely for Australia, but it's impact remains uncertain
item.description: W﻿inter and spring weather across multiple states and territories could be turned on their head, forecasters say.

item.title: US soldier arrested over $573,000 bet on capture of Venezuelan president
item.description: A special forces soldier involved in the operation to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro has been charged after he allegedly made a bet on the operation ahead of time.

item.title: Meta to slash 8000 jobs as tech giant embarks on aggressive restructure
item.description: The Mark Zuckerberg-founded company plans to cut its global workforce by 10 per cent﻿ and will cancel around 6000 open positions as Meta offsets its AI spend in a ruthless pursuit of "efficiency".

item.title: Trump, Kennedy double down on their impossible mathematics claims
item.description: Donald Trump and his Health Secretary Robert F Kennedy Jr have doubled down on their mathematically incorrect claims about how percentages work.

item.title: State guide: Businesses staying open over ANZAC Day weekend
item.description: While essential retailers will remain open,﻿ different states have their own unique restrictions on businesses.

item.title: Dingoes attack young girl and mother at Western Australia campsite
item.description: Rangers are patrolling with a licence to kill as the problem animals remain at large.

item.title: Drug testing ramps up in Australian 'white-collar' workplaces amid surge in cocaine detection
item.description: Positive workplace tests for the illegal party drug were up 45 per cent in the March quarter, according to the country's peak drug testing agency.

item.title: Can King Charles's visit rebuild UK ties with Trump?
item.description: T﻿he president's immense fondness for the British royal family may pay off when the monarch visits the US.

item.title: Mattress giant Emma Sleep ordered to pay $15 million over 'misleading' online ads
item.description: The "bed-in-a-box" retailer was found to have made false or misleading representations about the sale price of mattresses, bed frames, pillows and sleep accessories.

item.title: Guests praise "superhuman" efforts to keep them safe as major fire tore through Bali resort restaurant
item.description: Guests praised the "superhuman" efforts of staff to ensure guests were kept safe.

item.title: Man charged after allegedly biting, kicking flight attendant and passenger on Aussie flight
item.description: A man will face court in Perth today after allegedly kicking at a flight attendant and biting a fellow passenger on a flight from Canberra to Perth earlier this month.

item.title: Four 'finfluencers' targeted as warnings issued for TikTok money gurus
item.description: O﻿nline "finfluencers" are under examination amid fears they're doling out deceptive or unlicensed advice.

item.title: 'Shoot and kill': Trump's death orders issued
item.description: T﻿rump has ordered the Navy to fire "without hesitation" if approached.

item.title: Brazen petrol thief leaves service station owner fuming
item.description: The owner of a service station near Penrith has been left fuming after a truck driver stole close to $3000 worth of petrol in one hit.

item.title: 'Don't believe the bullshit': Sandilands tells reporters 'wait to see what comes out in court'
item.description: Shock jock Kyle Sandilands has told reporters "don't believe the bullshit" as his high-stakes court case continues to unfold.

item.title: Alcohol factory goes up in flames as police piece together new crime syndicate
item.description: A new organised crime racket is believed to be behind a spree of attacks on Melbourne hospitality venues.

item.title: Rebel accused of leaking actor's nude photo amid feud
item.description: A rising star who is suing Rebel Wilson claims the Hollywood star was behind a hack on her social media account that led to a nude photo being leaked.

item.title: Boy who killed Queensland grandmother Vyleen White in car jacking loses appeal
item.description: A teenager who killed a grandmother and triggered landmark youth justice laws has lost an appeal over his 16-year sentence.

item.title: Sniper who gunned down bikie boss unmasked by court
item.description: The hired sniper convicted of murdering a bikie leader at a drag racing event with a long-range shot can be identified after a gag order was lifted.

item.title: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu reveals he quietly underwent treatment for undisclosed prostate cancer
item.description: Netanyahu, 76, said he delayed the release of his annual medical report – which revealed he had been diagnosed with cancer for the first time – by two months so that it wouldn't be used as propaganda by Iran.

item.title: Adelaide family forced to crowdfund boy's life-saving cancer therapy after unfulfilled promise
item.description: An Adelaide family has been forced to crowdfund to send their child abroad for life-saving cancer therapy after an unfulfilled promise to bring the treatment to South Australia.

item.title: The Australian soldiers who fought epic battle in the 'forgotten war'
item.description: T﻿oday's Anzac Day falls 75 years after Australia's bloodiest battle of the Korean War.

item.title: Today in History - April 25: Murder charges after shark vomits up Sydney man's arm
item.description: Spectators at the Coogee Aquarium Baths in Sydney were horrified when a shark on display vomited up a human arm on April 25, 1935.

item.title: I found Australia's most secretive town. Here's why no one would talk to me
item.description: N﻿o one would talk to me for one specific reason - salt.

item.title: Cost-of-living hack? Inside the popular warehouses selling food past its best-before date
item.description: As the cost-of-living crisis deepens, a growing number of Australians are turning away from Coles and Woolworths in a bid to get better bargains elsewhere.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith attends Gold Coast Anzac Day service
item.description: Former SAS soldier Ben Roberts-Smith has attended the Anzac Day Currumbin Dawn Service on the Gold Coast.

item.title: Partial remains of Australian soldiers laid to rest more than a century after WWI
item.description: The partial remains of eight Australian soldiers have been laid to rest in France ahead of ANZAC Day, more than a century on from World War I.

item.title: Entertainment news headlines - 9News
item.description: Breaking entertainment news including celebrity gossip, film and movie news, music news, award ceremonies, scandals, photos and videos

item.title: Latest technology news headlines, updates & new tech innovation | 9News
item.description: Latest technology news stories including consumer electronics, science and space technology, gadgets, internet tech, medical devices, websites and social tech, cryptocurrencies and other technology news headlines

item.title: Latest health news top stories & analysis
item.description: Latest health news, research and results, scientific discoveries, diets, obesity, cancer, medical procedures and medicine, hospitals, and government health guidelines

item.title: Business news headlines - 9News
item.description: Latest business news, company tax, government regulations, CEOs, entrepreneurs, customers, and big business strategy

item.title: Politics News - 9News - Latest political news today Australia and World
item.description: Latest political news from Australia and around the world, covering elections, by-elections, our regular leadership spills, poll results, Question Time debates and daily Australian party politics

item.title: World news headlines and international current affairs - 9News
item.description: Latest world news headlines: International breaking news and current affairs from US, Europe, Middle East, Asia, Africa and more. If it happens around the world, you will know about it on 9News

item.title: News Australia - Latest news headlines in Australia
item.description: Today's news in major cities, regional and local areas which can include accident reports, police & emergency responses, criminal and court proceedings or live breaking news

item.title: 'Appalling': Boos mar Sydney, Melbourne Anzac Day dawn services
item.description: Australians have been gathering in their thousands around the country to mark Anzac Day and pay tribute to Australia's veteran community at ceremonies and events.

item.title: Trump sends son-in-law to negotiate with Iran, JD Vance not included
item.description: Donald Trump has sent his son-in-law to lead negotiations with an Iranian delegation in Pakistan.

item.title: 'Sinister, threatening, vulgar': jail for sextortion
item.description: A man who sent sexual AI-manipulated images to women in an attempt to extort real explicit photos from them has been jailed for more than two years.

item.title: US Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
item.description: The Justice Department will adopt firing squads as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases, officials said.

item.title: Alleged arson attack on Melbourne CBD bar
item.description: Three people have been arrested after an alleged arson attack on an iconic Melbourne CBD bar.
